regions.name: Crownhaven Reach
regions.description: The walled royal seat, wheat terraces and river docks radiating out from the throne.

regions.name: The Northmarch
regions.description: A frozen frontier of watch-forts and border levies holding the line against the Dragonspine.

regions.name: Aurelian Spire
regions.description: A vertical mage-city of floating study-halls and arcane markets built around a spire of white stone.

regions.name: Sylvewood Realm
regions.description: An ancient forest kingdom of tree-throne courts, older than Crownhaven's crown.

regions.name: Valdrakon Empire
regions.description: The southern empire's garrison provinces, all iron roads and legion camps.

regions.name: Saltmere Coast
regions.description: A free port coastline of smugglers, tide markets, and one very old drowned temple.

regions.name: Dragonspine Range
regions.description: A jagged mountain wall where wyrms still nest above the tree line.

regions.name: The Shattered Front
regions.description: A contested war border where Aurelian and Valdrakon claims still bleed into each other.

regions.name: The Deep Fen
regions.description: A sunken marsh riddled with sinkholes leading down into an older, buried city.

regions.name: Cinder Wastes
regions.description: A grey, ash-blown expanse past the old maps' edge. No expedition has returned with a full survey.

locations.name: Crownhaven Palace
locations.description: Seat of the Crown, its terraced gardens overlooking the river docks.

locations.name: Ironward Keep
locations.description: A frontier fortress garrisoned against raids out of the Dragonspine foothills.

locations.name: Frostfang Watchtower
locations.description: A lone signal tower on the last ridge before wyrm territory.

locations.name: The Aurelian Arcanum
locations.description: A floating academy of battle-mages and cartographers, chartered to map the Dragonspine.

locations.name: Sylvewood Grove-Throne
locations.description: The living-tree court of the Sylvan monarchs, grown rather than built.

locations.name: Whisperwood Shrine
locations.description: A moss-covered shrine said to answer questions asked at dusk.

locations.name: Valdrakon Citadel
locations.description: The empire's southern seat, ringed by three legion camps.

locations.name: Saltmere Free Docks
locations.description: A no-flag harbor where smugglers, refugees, and treasure-hunters all dock together.

locations.name: Sunken Temple of Vael
locations.description: A drowned temple visible only at low tide, its inner sanctum still dry after a thousand years.

locations.name: Dragonfang Aerie
locations.description: A wyrm's nesting cliff, littered with the wreckage of past expeditions.

locations.name: The Forbidden Vault
locations.description: A sealed pre-war ruin both sides claim and neither has taken.

locations.name: Catacombs of Kharon
locations.description: A flooded burial labyrinth beneath the Fen, older than any kingdom on the surface map.

locations.name: Fenmarsh Waystation
locations.description: A stilt-built trade post at the only dry crossing through the Fen.

locations.name: Last Watch Outpost
locations.description: A half-buried watch camp at the edge of the surveyed world. Reports from it have grown rare.

factions.name: Crownhaven Crown
factions.description: The ruling kingdom holding the map's center.

factions.name: Aurelian Concord
factions.description: A neutral academy-state that sells maps and wards to whoever pays.

factions.name: Sylvewood Court
factions.description: The old forest court, allied to the Crown by treaty.

factions.name: Valdrakon Empire
factions.description: An expanding southern empire pressing the Shattered Front.

factions.name: Saltmere Free Traders
factions.description: An unaligned merchant council that trades with every flag.

loreHistory.event: The old empire that built the Forbidden Vault and the Catacombs of Kharon collapsed; both Crownhaven and Valdrakon claim its ruins.
The Shattered Front opened when Aurelian and Valdrakon surveyors' claims overlapped on the same ruin.
The last full survey team into the Cinder Wastes stopped reporting.

initialNpcs.name: Queen Isolde
initialNpcs.description: The current Crown, holding the center against two hostile borders.

initialNpcs.name: Archmagister Venn
initialNpcs.description: Head cartographer of the Arcanum, selling Dragonspine survey maps to the highest bidder.
